name: SOL Range Failure Reacceptance v1
thesis: In a mixed chop/transition regime, SOL 4h should produce tradable failed range escapes that quickly re-enter value. The edge is not trend prediction; it is monetizing rejection of failed directional acceptance back toward balance.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed range escapes are too sparse, if re-entry bars do not lead to value travel after costs, or if SOL behaves as persistent breakout tape instead of rejection-repair tape.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Multi-bar push outside short-term balance loses acceptance, then price re-enters balance and mean reverts far enough to pay costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Choppy regimes create false escapes and trapped late entrants. Reacceptance after a failed escape should be denser than continuation-only logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h offers enough volatility for balance failures without the micro-noise of 1h. It is the cleanest primary lane for this mechanism in the current basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If escapes persist instead of failing, reacceptance entries will be steamrolled.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries trigger with density but follow-through is too shallow, reduce target ambition before touching the reacceptance trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_trade_density: Moderate; failed range escapes should appear more often than deep capitulation or full trend resets.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Modest win rate with small-to-medium mean reversion payouts and limited holding times; best trades should resolve within a few bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Screen shows credible density and positive post-cost mean reversion behavior across at least the primary lane and one confirmation lane.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repeated fake reacceptance that immediately reverses back out of balance.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; excursion and re-entry are sequential rather than mutually restrictive
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: controlled with moderate ATR thresholds instead of rare extreme breaks

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for the current hypothesis because balance failures should be visible and frequent enough on SOL 4h.
validation_basket.continue_if: Credible trade density with positive post-cost screen behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or persistent breakout behavior overwhelms mean reversion.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent failed escapes that travel back toward EMA_20/EMA_55 value quickly.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism is structural rather than SOL-specific noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: The same broad reacceptance logic trades with similar density and cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Mechanism disappears entirely or only works on one volatile lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly lower volatility but cleaner balance-failure repair than SOL.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism survives faster structure without collapsing into noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: Density rises without destroying edge quality.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Noise dominates and false reentries explode.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More signals than 4h, but still enough value repair to support smaller targets.
